tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Cancer survivor claims Tour de France Yellow Jersey at 30
stories.summary: Torstein Traeen, 30, wears cycling's most prestigious yellow jersey at the Tour de France, four years after testicular cancer diagnosis discovered during a 2022 doping test.

stories.headline: 'Our little miracle' returns to the field after cancer and amputation
stories.summary: Twelve-year-old Rhealynn Mills returns to the softball field after right leg amputation and multiple surgeries for osteosarcoma—her family calls her 'our little miracle.'

stories.headline: One family's free lunch box becomes a neighborhood's lifeline
stories.summary: Rhonda and Craig prepare 25 free lunches daily, feeding disabled neighbors who arrive each day with grateful hearts and hope.

stories.headline: First patients receive hope-bringing stem cell treatment for Parkinson's
stories.summary: Eight Parkinson's patients receive transplanted dopamine cells with no serious side effects, offering real possibility of restoring lost brain function.

stories.headline: Man spends entire day helping storm-damaged neighbors ask for nothing
stories.summary: Johnny Harris restores faith in human kindness, spending hours helping neighbors clean up storm damage with his own equipment and hands.

stories.headline: Softball star returns to play months after completing cancer treatment
stories.summary: Paige Sinicki, 22, takes the field for the Portland Cascade just eight months after completing chemotherapy, surgery, and radiation for Stage 2 breast cancer.

stories.headline: Endangered wild sheep nearly double in Pakistan's mountains
stories.summary: Punjab urial herds have nearly doubled across Pakistan's 300-square-kilometre Salt Range, with recent footage showing the endangered wild sheep roaming freely near the Chakwal motorway.

stories.headline: Surgeons perform world-first operation using remotely controlled humanoid robots
stories.summary: Surgeons remotely controlled humanoid robots to remove gallbladders from live pigs in a world-first operation, offering a fraction-of-the-cost alternative to expensive surgical robots.

stories.headline: 79 endangered sea turtle hatchlings released into the ocean
stories.summary: Alert citizens helped rescue and release 79 hawksbill sea turtle hatchlings along Mexico's Yucatan coast after spotting them near 71st street in Progreso.

stories.headline: Relief volunteers commit to stay until every flooded home is restored
stories.summary: Samaritan's Purse Canada vowed never to leave Swan River until completing all work orders, with program manager Andy Northup saying they'd received 85 requests as of Thursday.

stories.headline: New robot flies and swims to monitor ocean ecosystems
stories.summary: MIT engineers created a half-pound robot inspired by Atlantic puffins that launches from water in under a second to monitor ocean ecosystems and coastal health.

stories.headline: Extinct singing insect's eggs brought home to England after 30 years
stories.summary: British conservationists retrieved New Forest Cicada eggs from a French military base in Brittany after the singing insect vanished from England in the 1990s.

stories.headline: Giant solar plant powers homes after dark using molten salt
stories.summary: World's largest solar hybrid plant stores sunlight as molten salt, generating clean power for eight hours after sunset and serving 830,000 homes yearly.

stories.headline: Earth's mangrove forests make unexpected comeback after decades of loss
stories.summary: Global mangrove forests show unexpected net gains after decades of loss, with restoration efforts reversing devastation documented between 2000-2020.
